title: Ethnic Advocacy Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Ethnic Advocacy Groups 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to enhance understanding of Islam, encourage dialogue, protect civil liberties, empower American Muslims, and build coalitions that promote justice and mutual understanding. Both the national and chapter offices field reports of hate crimes, harassment, and other forms of discrimination related to being Muslim. Issues action alerts to generate grassroots responses to political, social, and media-related issues. Various CAIR publications, including an annual civil rights report, are available online. CAIR and its chapters regularly sponsor voter registration drives across the country to increase Muslim participation in the political arena. Organizes conferences and seminars about Islam and Muslims for government and law enforcement agencies, media professionals, and the academic community. Diversity training workshops are offered for employers, educators, and organizations. 453 New Jersey Avenue SE Washington, DC 20003 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (202) 488-8787 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to enhance understanding of Islam, encourage dialogue, protect civil liberties, empower American Muslims, and build coalitions that promote justice and mutual understanding. Both the national and chapter offices field reports of hate crimes, harassment, and other forms of discrimination related to being Muslim. Issues action alerts to generate grassroots responses to political, social, and media-related issues. Various CAIR publications, including an annual civil rights report, are available online. CAIR and its chapters regularly sponsor voter registration drives across the country to increase Muslim participation in the political arena. Organizes conferences and seminars about Islam and Muslims for government and law enforcement agencies, media professionals, and the academic community. Diversity training workshops are offered for employers, educators, and organizations. Website Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LATINO NETWORK ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trains youth and adults in civic engagement and advocacy, mobilizes community members for key meetings and testimony opportunities, and advocates at the city, county, and state level. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 283-6881 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOMALI AMERICAN COUNCIL OF OREGON EAST AFRICAN AND SOMALI CULTURALLY SPECIFIC SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and community services to the African immigrant and refugee community. 12409 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 719-7679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (503) 287-4117 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON UNITE OREGON ROGUE VALLEY CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (503) 828-9736 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND ADVOCACY AND CIVIC ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to advance the organization’s social policy agenda to create institutional change in areas that most deeply affect African Americans, communities of color, and others. Urban League of Portland works with state legislature, executive branch and city councils, school boards, and county commissions to identify policy change opportunities. Conducts policy research and writing, develops issue campaigns and messaging, mobilizes constituencies and coalitions, and works with coalition partners as well as decision-makers. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS COUNCIL ON AMERICAN-ISLAMIC RELATIONS OREGON LATINO NETWORK ADVOCACY SOMALI AMERICAN COUNCIL OF OREGON EAST AFRICAN AND SOMALI CULTURALLY SPECIFIC SERVICES UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND ADVOCACY AND CIVIC ENGAGEMENT
